html:
At IDR 4,000, PT Baramulti Suksessarana is not the negative-enterprise-value coal story in the older draft. The better thesis is cleaner: BSSR is a debt-free, cash-generative Indonesian thermal coal producer whose 2025 downcycle is already being misread as the new normal just as Q1 2026 earnings re-accelerated and Indonesian benchmark coal prices turned higher.

The stock closed at IDR 4,000 on May 18, 2026, with a market cap near IDR 10.45 trillion. FY2025 revenue fell to US$703.8 million and net income to US$84.0 million, but Q1 2026 revenue rose 9.5% year over year to US$167.7 million and net income rose 30.5% to US$25.5 million. That puts BSSR on roughly US$90 million TTM earnings, or about 7x earnings, and nearer 6x if Q1 is annualized. Operating cash flow remains stronger than earnings: FY2025 operating cash flow after royalties and tax was US$161.6 million, and Q1 2026 added US$52.3 million.

The balance sheet is real protection, though not the old overstated version. At March 2026, BSSR had US$61.0 million cash, another US$43.7 million restricted cash, and no bank debt. Cash and restricted cash together are roughly 16% of the market cap. The company is not cheap only because of trapped cash. It is cheap because current-cycle free cash flow can plausibly run US$120 million to US$160 million, putting the equity at roughly 4 to 5x forward FCF before any coal-price upside.

The variant perception is that the market sees “terminal coal, lower 2025 earnings, Indonesia governance discount.” That is not entirely wrong, but it is incomplete. BSSR is still moving large volumes through two concessions, AGM in South Kalimantan and BSSR’s IUP in East Kalimantan, and sells into the Asian demand pool where India, China, Southeast Asia, Korea, Japan, Taiwan, and the Philippines still matter. The key current fact is that coal pricing is no longer deteriorating. Indonesia’s 6,322 kcal GAR HBA rose from US$106.57/t in early May to US$116.32/t for the second half of May 2026, the third consecutive increase. BSSR’s Q1 margin recovery happened before that full price move showed up.

The dividend mechanism is the second reason this can re-rate. BSSR paid IDR 473.82 per share over the last twelve months, an 11.8% trailing yield at IDR 4,000. For FY2025, it already paid two interim dividends totaling US$55 million, and a final 2025 dividend remains a live catalyst if the company keeps the historical high-payout pattern. Indonesian retail yield investors already know BSSR as a dividend name, but the stock still has no meaningful institutional coverage and screens like a melting coal stub rather than a live cash-return vehicle.

The governance discount is deserved. Related-party marketing fees, hauling and logistics arrangements, Indonesian royalty changes, DMO exposure, and thin liquidity all matter. The 2025 regulatory changes also make benchmark coal prices more important for both transaction floors and state take, so a coal rally is not all pure margin. Still, the offset is unusually tangible: cash is not just being hoarded, and shareholders have repeatedly been paid.

The base case is not heroic. Assume coal stays around current-cycle Asian levels, BSSR earns US$100 million to US$115 million, pays out 65% to 85% of earnings, and the market values it at 8x to 9x earnings because the dividend remains visible. That supports IDR 6,500, or 63% upside from IDR 4,000, before dividends.

Bear case: IDR 2,900, down 28%. Coal rolls over, realized prices compress, the government take rises, and earnings fall toward US$45 million to US$55 million. The dividend is cut and the stock trades at 5x to 6x trough earnings, partly cushioned by cash and no bank debt.

Base case: IDR 6,500, up 63%. Q1 2026 proves sustainable, May HBA strength flows through, earnings recover above US$100 million, and the dividend yield normalizes toward 7% to 8%.

Bull case: IDR 9,500, up 138%. Asian coal tightens, BSSR earns US$150 million plus, FCF exceeds US$170 million, and the company pays another large special or semiannual dividend. At only 8x bull earnings, with cash still on the balance sheet, the stock can more than double.
